question: [File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getInstance()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger fee estimation before @PostConstruct init() completes, causing getInstance() to throw IllegalStateException and bypass fee validation entirely, resulting in transactions being processed with no fee context applied?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getInstance()] Can an unprivileged external user race a concurrent Spring context refresh against getInstance() so that INSTANCE.get() returns a stale CommonProperties with realm=0/shard=0 while the live network uses non-zero values, causing FeeEstimationFeeContext to compute fees against the wrong network shard and undercharge transaction fees?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: init()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a high-volume burst of fee-estimation API requests during application startup before init() sets INSTANCE, causing fee context to be constructed with null shard/realm and producing zero-fee or incorrect-fee estimates for all in-flight transactions?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Field: realm] Can an unprivileged external user craft an API request supplying a realm value that overflows the @Min(0) long boundary (e.g., Long.MAX_VALUE) so that EntityId.of(shard, realm, num) produces a negative encoded ID, causing fee collector account lookups to resolve to wrong accounts and assessed custom fees to be credited to attacker-controlled entities?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Field: shard] Can an unprivileged external user supply an arbitrarily large shard value (no upper bound enforced beyond @Min(0)) through a public API endpoint that reads CommonProperties.getInstance().getShard(), causing fee estimation in FeeEstimationFeeContext to use a mismatched hedera.shard config and produce fees outside the design parameters of the target network?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Field: realm] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a @Max constraint on realm to inject realm=Long.MAX_VALUE via an environment variable (HIERO_MIRROR_COMMON_REALM), causing FeeEstimationFeeContext's static CONFIG_PROVIDER to be initialized with an out-of-range realm, making all subsequent fee calculations use an invalid network context and bypassing fee schedule enforcement?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Field: shard] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the missing upper-bound validation on shard (only @Min(0) present) to set shard to a value that causes integer overflow in EntityId bit-packing used by fee collector resolution, resulting in custom fees being assessed against the wrong collector account ID?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getInstance()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a class-load of FeeEstimationFeeContext before CommonProperties.init() runs, causing the static CONFIG_PROVIDER field to capture shard=0/realm=0 permanently, so that all fee estimations for the lifetime of the JVM use the wrong network parameters regardless of the actual configured shard/realm?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Field: INSTANCE] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the AtomicReference<CommonProperties> INSTANCE singleton by triggering a Spring context reload (e.g., via a public actuator endpoint if misconfigured) that calls init() a second time with different realm/shard values, causing in-flight fee estimation requests to see inconsistent shard/realm mid-computation and produce fees outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: init()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that init() unconditionally overwrites INSTANCE via INSTANCE.set(this) without checking for an existing value, so that a second Spring bean initialization (e.g., via prototype scope misconfiguration) replaces the singleton mid-request, causing concurrent fee estimation threads to read inconsistent realm/shard values and compute incorrect fees?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Class: DatabaseStartupProperties] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the enabled=true default in DatabaseStartupProperties to cause the application to block indefinitely on database startup (by making the DB unreachable), preventing fee schedule data from being loaded into the mirror node and causing all fee estimations to fall back to zero or default values outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Field: timeout] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @DurationMin(seconds=10) lower bound on DatabaseStartupProperties.timeout by setting timeout=10s (minimum allowed), causing the database waiter to give up after only 10 seconds during a transient DB outage, leaving the fee schedule table unpopulated and all subsequent fee calculations using stale or empty fee data?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Field: connectTimeout] Can an unprivileged external user set DatabaseStartupProperties.connectTimeout to its minimum of 1 second via environment variable injection, causing JDBC connection attempts to time out before the database responds during high-load periods, preventing fee schedule updates from being persisted and causing the mirror node to serve stale fee data to clients?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Field: socketTimeout] Can an unprivileged external user set DatabaseStartupProperties.socketTimeout to 1 second (minimum allowed by @DurationMin(seconds=1)), causing JDBC socket reads to time out mid-query during fee schedule ingestion, resulting in partial fee schedule writes to the database and inconsistent fee data being served to downstream consumers?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Field: validationTimeout] Can an unprivileged external user set DatabaseStartupProperties.validationTimeout to Integer.MAX_VALUE seconds (allowed by @DurationMax(seconds=Integer.MAX_VALUE)), causing connection.isValid() in DatabaseWaiter to block for ~68 years, effectively freezing the application startup thread and preventing any fee schedule data from being loaded?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Field: interval] Can an unprivileged external user set DatabaseStartupProperties.interval to its minimum of 500ms via config injection, causing the database retry loop in DatabaseWaiter to spin at maximum frequency and exhaust database connection pool resources, starving fee schedule ingestion queries and causing fee data to be unavailable?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Field: realm] Can an unprivileged external user submit a REST API request with a crafted entity ID that bypasses the default realm=0 assumption in CommonProperties, causing the fee collector account ID to be resolved in a different realm than the one holding the actual fee schedule, resulting in fees being assessed against a non-existent or attacker-controlled account?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getInstance()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a NullPointerException chain by calling a fee-estimation endpoint that internally calls CommonProperties.getInstance() before Spring context initialization, causing the fee estimation service to crash and return a default zero-fee response to the caller, effectively bypassing fee enforcement?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Field: realm] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that realm has no @Max constraint and is a primitive long (not Long) to supply realm=9223372036854775807 (Long.MAX_VALUE) through a Spring Boot environment variable, causing EntityId.of(shard, realm, num) to produce a bit-packed ID that wraps around and maps to a fee collector account with num=0 (treasury), redirecting all custom fees to the treasury account?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Field: shard] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a @Max annotation on shard to inject shard=65536 (exceeding the 16-bit field width used in EntityId encoding), causing fee collector entity IDs to be computed with truncated shard bits, silently mapping fee payments to wrong accounts outside the design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Class: CommonProperties] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @Data Lombok annotation (which generates public setters for realm and shard) combined with a Spring Boot Actuator /env endpoint (if exposed without authentication) to dynamically change realm/shard at runtime, causing all subsequent fee estimations to use the attacker-chosen network parameters?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Field: databaseStartup] Can an unprivileged external user set databaseStartup.enabled=false via environment variable to disable the database startup wait, causing the application to start before fee schedule tables are populated, and serving zero or default fees for all transactions until the database catches up?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Field: connectTimeout] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @DurationUnit(ChronoUnit.SECONDS) annotation on connectTimeout to inject a fractional second value that Spring Boot's duration converter rounds down to 0, bypassing the @DurationMin(seconds=1) constraint and causing JDBC connections to have no timeout, enabling connection-hold attacks that starve fee schedule ingestion?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Field: interval] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the mismatch between @DurationMin(millis=500) and @DurationUnit(ChronoUnit.SECONDS) on DatabaseStartupProperties.interval — where the unit is seconds but the minimum is 500ms — to inject interval=0 which passes the millis check but results in a zero-second sleep in DatabaseWaiter, causing a tight retry loop that exhausts DB connections and prevents fee data from loading?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getInstance()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the IllegalStateException thrown by getInstance() when INSTANCE is null to cause a cascading failure in fee estimation services that catch RuntimeException broadly, silently returning a zero-fee estimate and allowing transactions to be submitted with fees below the network minimum?
